airplane:
The objects in focus are airplanes, typically found at airports. These aircraft are characterized by their elongated fuselage, wings extending from either side, and tail fins at the rear. They are often positioned on tarmac areas, either stationary or in motion, near airport terminals or runways. The airplanes vary in size and design, but all share the common features of a streamlined body, wing structures for lift, and a tail assembly for stability and control. 

Distinctive features that set these objects apart from other elements in the scene include their symmetrical shape, the presence of engines (usually under the wings or at the rear), and their placement in relation to airport infrastructure. Unlike other objects in the scene, such as buildings or vehicles, airplanes have a unique combination of these aerodynamic features and are the only objects capable of flight. This combination of visual characteristics makes it possible to accurately recognize and distinguish airplanes in future images, especially when they are situated in the context of an airport environment.
